refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into data acquisition and data processing steps, each contributing to total latency. This allows targeted design parameters to address each latency source.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the p95 latency of data acquisition and data processing separately, then combined, ensuring total update latency meets ≤1.0 s requirement.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

potential_sources: Data acquisition delays impacting processing start time
Processing algorithm complexity affecting update frequency
coupling_check.notes: DP1 and DP2 are logically coupled but can be modularized to minimize cross-dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry network supports required bandwidth and latency
Risk of processing algorithm complexity exceeding latency budget
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and optimize processing algorithms iteratively.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry data acquisition and real-time processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and vehicle data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Vehicle and Teleoperation for operational use

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and external modules for data exchange

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes update logic and data processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Primarily data source and actuator, less suitable for centralized update processing.
Focuses on operator control, not suitable for core update processing.
Focused on safety alerts, unrelated to update latency processing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency management within NGHE Solution module reduces diffusion and improves latency performance.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or distributed processing if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into acquisition and processing, and selected DPs logically address these sub-FRs.

diffusion_risks: Risk of over-centralization in NGHE Solution module causing performance bottlenecks
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as owner of update latency logic, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end and at module interfaces using Telemetry and Tasking interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update latency responsibility to NGHE Solution module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor telemetry network performance and processing load to avoid bottlenecks.
Refine processing algorithms if latency targets are not met.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and verifiable with clear ownership and interface definitions. No immediate need to revise or zigzag.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: Current level meets requirements and constraints with acceptable risk.

assumptions: Telemetry network supports required bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can be optimized to meet latency targets
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of telemetry data acquisition
System integration test results verifying end-to-end update latency
